symptom: a dull ache under the jaw that swelled up at every meal then settled an hour later for months
hook: the spit gland had grown a hard rock that was plugging the drain like a cork
whatYouSee: A bright white pebble parked under the jaw exactly where soft spit-gland tissue should be. A literal stone glowing inside a gland that only makes liquid.
whyItMatters: Saliva keeps backing up behind the stone so the gland swells and balloons at meals and can get infected which turns a nuisance ache into a hot abscess in the neck.
treatment: Small stones flush out with sour candy massage and fluids while big lodged ones are removed by a scope through the duct a small incision under the tongue or taking out the whole gland.
takeaway: A gland built to make pure liquid can mineralize its own saliva into a stone big enough to dam the flow and show up like a marble on a flat X-ray.
igOptions: A. A swallowed pebble stuck under the tongue
B. A stone the gland grew from its own saliva
C. A piece of broken tooth that migrated

condition.igTitle: THE STONE IN THE SPIT GLAND

A piece of broken tooth that migrated

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a dull ache under the jaw that swelled up at every meal then settled an hour later for months.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And the spit gland had grown a hard rock that was plugging the drain like a cork.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Giant submandibular sialolith

👀 What you see:
A bright white pebble parked under the jaw exactly where soft spit-gland tissue should be. A literal stone glowing inside a gland that only makes liquid.

🦴 Why it matters:
Saliva keeps backing up behind the stone so the gland swells and balloons at meals and can get infected which turns a nuisance ache into a hot abscess in the neck.
